name: French Defense MacCutcheon Variation
description: A sharp French line where Black immediately counterattacks White's bishop and center.
movesPreview: 1. e4 e6 2. d4 d5 3. Nc3 Nf6 4. Bg5 Bb4

name: French Defense Steinitz Variation
description: A classical French structure where White supports the center and prepares kingside development.

name: French Defense Exchange Variation
description: A simple French line where White exchanges early and aims for straightforward development.

name: Sicilian Defense O'Kelly Variation
description: A less common Sicilian line where Black plays ...a6 early to sidestep some anti-Sicilian setups.

name: Sicilian Defense Hyper-Accelerated Dragon
description: A very fast Dragon setup where Black fianchettos immediately and pressures the center later.

name: Sicilian Defense Canal Attack
description: A strategic anti-Sicilian where White develops with Bb5 and tries to damage Black's structure.

name: King's Indian Defense Averbakh Variation
description: A restrained King's Indian system where White uses Be2 and Bg5 ideas to limit Black's counterplay.
movesPreview: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Be2

name: King's Indian Defense Petrosian Variation
description: A slower anti-King's Indian setup where White aims for control and reduced kingside danger.
movesPreview: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 Bg7 4. e4 d6 5. Nf3 O-O 6. Be2 e5 7. d5

name: Grunfeld Defense Russian System
description: A main anti-Grunfeld system where White develops solidly and fights to maintain the center.
movesPreview: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 g6 3. Nc3 d5 4. Nf3

name: Benko Gambit Accepted
description: A major Benko line where White accepts the gambit and Black gets long-term queenside pressure.
movesPreview: 1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 c5 3. d5 b5 4. cxb5 a6

name: English Opening Botvinnik System
description: A strategic English setup with g3, Bg2, e4 and Nge2 ideas leading to rich maneuvering play.
movesPreview: 1. c4 Nf6 2. Nc3 e6 3. g3 d5 4. Bg2

name: Réti Opening King's Indian Attack
description: A flexible system opening where White uses Nf3, g3 and Bg2 against many black setups.

name: Blackmar-Diemer Gambit
description: A sharp gambit where White sacrifices a pawn early for open lines and rapid development.

name: Stonewall Attack
description: A setup opening where White builds a Stonewall structure with pawns on d4, e3, f4 and c3.
